methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Monumo, a Series B chip design company founded by Martin Frost and Ian Murphy, is evaluated against a universe of 8 private peers and 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD). The target has raised $100M to date and operates in a sector where private valuations currently range from $500M to $5.0B across early and growth stages.

Two valuation methods triangulate the range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $100M total raised proxy, yielding $190M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies (Etched, d-Matrix, Positron AI, Substrate, DEEPX, and one additional peer) establishes a $1.0B median with a $500M–$5.0B spread. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 20.6x) is not applicable due to absence of disclosed revenue for the target.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $162M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $595M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around whether Monumo's stage progression and technology maturity align closer to early-stage peers or later-stage, higher-valued chip design competitors. Revenue traction, technical differentiation, and pathway to commercialization remain critical valuation sensitivities not resolved by available data.
